trust_context: The team wants AI help reducing intake/admin drag without making new clients feel processed by a machine during the first trust-building moments.

surface_steps.step: Collect basic contact and scheduling details
surface_steps.reason: This is repeatable machine-work when the client has a clear path to human help.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: Annoyance if the form is long, broken, or hides a human contact option.

surface_steps.step: Summarize client context before first meeting
surface_steps.current_owner: Intake coordinator with account owner review
surface_steps.reason: AI can reduce prep time, but a human should verify tone, omissions, and sensitive details.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: Wrong emphasis or missed nuance could shape the first human conversation poorly.

surface_steps.step: Acknowledge anxiety, urgency, or sensitive circumstances
surface_steps.reason: Presence and accountability are the premium; the client needs to feel met, not routed.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: Trust loss, perceived indifference, or accidental mishandling of sensitive context.

surface_steps.step: Decide whether the client needs escalation or special handling
surface_steps.reason: AI can flag possible issues, but responsibility must stay with a named human owner.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: Unaccountable triage, inappropriate routing, or false confidence.

ai_roles: Draft intake summaries for human review
Prepare a private checklist for the first meeting
Never send sensitive reassurance or escalation decisions without human approval
protected_human_work: Promises about outcomes, timing, cost, or obligations
human_checkpoints: Named owner reviews all AI-generated intake summaries before use
Client has a visible human contact path at every step
Escalation flags go to a human before any client-facing action
Pilot review after 10 onboarding cases or 30 days, whichever comes first
boundary_notes: Review aid only; not legal, medical, employment, compliance, or financial advice.
Actual workflow changes require the domain owner to approve the policy and scripts.
AI removes admin drag; it does not replace the trust-bearing human relationship.
